thread.title: Glasgow marchers brave storm to demand no arms sales to Israel
thread.title_full: Glasgow marchers brave storm to demand no arms sales to Israel

author: TTE
title: Glasgow marchers brave storm to demand no arms sales to Israel
text:
On the march against war and imperialism in Glasgow
“Come rain, come snow, Israeli terror has got to go,” protesters chanted as they marched through the centre of Glasgow on Saturday.
Storm Bert didn’t deter the 1,000 people who turned out on the national demonstration. It was called by Stop The War Scotland , the Scottish Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament (CND) and the STUC trade union federation.
Protestor Maisie said, “Even when the weather’s like this, it is so important to come out and show solidarity. Over a year and we still have to come and demonstrate, it’s ridiculous—the government needs to listen to us.”
Lynn Jamieson, Scottish CND chair, told Socialist Worker, “Today we were calling on a complete ban on arms to Israel and for the British government to speak up and clearly name Netanyahu as a war criminal.
“What’s happening on Gaza is genocide and we want our government to acknowledge that it should have acted much sooner.”
The ICC court, based in the Hague in the Netherlands, released arrest warrants for Israeli prime minister Binyamin Netanyahu and former defence minister Yoav Gallant on Thursday. Protesters chanted, “Let’s be clear, don’t be vague, Netanyahu in the Hague.”
Some protesters weren’t convinced that the ICC’s ruling would change anything. David said, “Nothing is being done and there should be more. The ICC won’t do a thing—I think they’re all corrupt at this point.”
Maisie added, “Even with the ICC ruling, I doubt anything will change with Starmer. It’s why we’re here—we have to force change, we have to fight.”
Protester Jess thought that “the pressure we have put on our governments is what’s forcing them to make concessions”. “If the movement can escalate then we keep holding our governments to account,” she said.
“That’s essential because that’s what helped to push the ICC to send out the arrest warrants.”
Protester Alex said, “We have to recognise that our governments have a role to play because they keep funding genocide. They’re escalating the wars, so we need to escalate our actions. We need to bring more people, we need to bring more action and more occupations.
“It should lift all of us that there are this many people who want to stand in solidarity with Palestinians.”
Another protester, David, said, “We need to bring them to a standstill. To me, it feels like more direct action is needed. Big demos like today are important and it’s a good starting point.
“But to stop them from supporting genocide and trading arms, we need to bring them to a standstill. So we have to escalate action.”
After marching through Glasgow, protesters held a rally. Keir McKechnie from Stop The War Scotland, warned the rally about how Western imperialism is ramping up the drive to war.
“This situation is extremely dangerous—Gaza, the extension of Israel’s war into Lebanon, the threat of war with Iran and the crisis in Ukraine,” he said.
“Joe Biden and Keir Starmer are willing to see as many Russians and Ukrainians die for imperialism. But it’s not in the interests of workers in Russia and Ukraine to fight a proxy war.
“It’s critical that we deepen the movement in the trade unions because that’s where we can target the profiteers of blood money.”
The Stop The War workplace day of action on Thursday 28 November is an opportunity to deepen the anti-war movement in the unions.
Janette Finchley, president of the UCU university and colleges union in Scotland, said, “People work everywhere and there’s always something you can do. If you can, do any small thing in your workplace.
“Glasgow university was told that people wanted disinvestment but it has refused. It refused to do so for an explicitly political reason.
“it doesn’t want to give the impression that the university is against military activity. We need to put pressure on it.”
Lawyer Aamer Anwar said, “It will take a global movement to defeat Israel so today we are sending a message of solidarity to all facing Israel.
“We will not be silenced on calling out the genocide. The word that they tried to ban resonates with people of the Global South, of Africa and of the Middle East because of the battles they have waged against their oppressors.”
Stop The War vice chair Chris Nineham closed the rally. He said, “What we are doing is confronting the most powerful power on earth in one of the key planks of its imperial project.
“As well as the Palestine movement, we have to build a wider movement against war and militarism. We have to be prepared for the unthinkable, protesting now against the catastrophe that they are risking.”
The message on the march was clear—expand the Palestine movement, deepen it in the trade unions and resist the brutalities of war and imperialism.
